channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Biomarkers & Precision Medicine
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Biomarkers & Precision Medicine

item.title: Smartly engineered biomaterials drive immune remodeling: A new paradigm for precise treatment of inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: International immunopharmacology - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a group of autoimmune disorders characterized by chronic and relapsing intestinal inflammation. Although drug therapy can alleviate clinical symptoms to some ext...

item.title: PRAS40-driven pro-inflammatory macrophage polarization is required for inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Cell death & disease - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs) are chronic inflammatory disorders with unclear pathogenesis and limited therapeutic options. Macrophage dysfunction is central to IBD progression, yet how metabo...

item.title: Temporal transcriptomic profiling identifies core regulators in cytokine-induced gut barrier disruption in differentiated Caco-2 cells.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - The intestinal epithelial barrier maintains gut homeostasis, and its disruption contributes to inflammatory diseases such as Inflammatory Bowel Diseases (IBD). Although barrier restoration is a pro...

item.title: Epigenetic reprogramming of tissue-resident memory T cells in chronic inflammatory disorders and implications for targeted therapies.
item.description: Epigenomics - Tissue-resident memory T (TRM) cells play a role in causing long-term tissue injury in chronic inflammatory diseases via pathological epigenetic reprogramming. Nevertheless, the epigenetic processe...

item.title: The role and targeting potential analysis of angiogenesis-related target THY1 in DSS-induced acute colitis in mice.
item.description: PloS one - Immune-driven inflammatory angiogenesis plays a crucial role in the pathogenesis of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, the mechanism of chronic inflammation mediated by angiogenesis still r...

item.title: Unveiling the Molecular Repertoire of Akkermansia muciniphila: From Mechanistic Insights to Precision Biotherapeutics.
item.description: Journal of microbiology and biotechnology - Review - The gut microbiota has been established as a cornerstone of host physiological homeostasis. Among its diverse members, Akkermansia muciniphila has gained significant prominence as a premier next-ge...

item.title: Diagnostic Accuracy of Salivary Periodontal Biomarkers Is Reduced in Patients With Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Clinical and experimental dental research - Patients with Crohn's disease (CD) are at increased risk of developing periodontal disease and may benefit from salivary biomarker-based monitoring for early detection and management. Given the pre...

item.title: Colonic epithelial regeneration shapes susceptibility to Clostridioides difficile infection.
item.description: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology - Clostridioides difficile infection (CDI) susceptibility and severity are strongly associated with preexisting colonic inflammation. However, chronic inflammatory conditions such as cystic fibrosis ...

item.title: Bone targeted microenvironment actuated engineered exosomes for precision therapy of IBD associated bone loss.
item.description: Bioactive materials - Systemic bone loss is a frequent extraintestinal complication of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and can continue even when intestinal inflammation is clinically controlled. The steps linking gut ...

item.title: From dysbiosis to precision medicine: targeting the microbial-metabolic axis in IBD management.
item.description: Frontiers in cellular and infection microbiology - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic relapsing inflammatory condition that has a rapidly changing global epidemiology. IBD has been traditionally viewed as a primary immune system dysfunct...

item.title: Gegen Qinlian Decoction Attenuates Ulcerative Colitis by Inhibiting PANoptosis: A Multi-Omics and Experimental Study.
item.description: Journal of visualized experiments : JoVE - We present an integrated bioinformatics and network pharmacology protocol to clarify the therapeutic mechanisms of Gegen Qinlian Decoction (GQD) in ulcerative colitis (UC), with a particular focus ...

item.title: STRIDE-II treatment target achievement in inflammatory bowel disease: a cross-sectional study from a tertiary IBD centre in the United Arab Emirates.
item.description: Saudi journal of gastroenterology : official journal of the Saudi Gastroenterology Association - Data on STRIDE-II treatment target achievement in Middle Eastern inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) populations remain limited. We assessed the prevalence and predictors of deep remission at a tertia...
